1029	  The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}),
1030	  together with the tautological quotient \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\)
1031	  (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}), represents the functor
1032	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}). That is, for
1033	  every scheme \(T\) the natural map
1034	  \[
1035	    \operatorname{Hom}(T, \mathrm{Gr}(r,d))
1036	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
1037	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T),
1038	    \qquad
1039	    \bigl(\,\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\,\bigr)
1040	      \;\longmapsto\;
1041	    \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U},\, \varphi^{*}u \rangle,
1042	  \]
1043	  is a bijection, natural in \(T\). Consequently the Quot scheme
1044	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
1045	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) of rank-\(d\) quotients of the trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf
1046	  exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\); the tautological quotient is the
1047	  universal family.

1050	\begin{proof}
1051	  \uses{def:tautological_quotient, def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf,
1052	    def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix,
1053	    def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme,
1054	    def:grassmannian_functor}
1055	  Naturality of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u
1056	  \rangle\) is immediate: pullback is functorial and \(\varphi^{*}u\) is again a
1057	  surjection onto a rank-\(d\) locally free sheaf, so the assignment defines a
1058	  morphism of functors \(\operatorname{Hom}(-,\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)) \to
1059	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\). It remains to show it is a bijection for each
1060	  \(T\); we exhibit an inverse, constructed Zariski-locally on \(T\) and glued.
1061	
1062	  \emph{From a quotient to a morphism.} Let \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle \in
1063	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)\), so \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow
1064	  \mathcal{F}\) with \(\mathcal{F}\) locally free of rank \(d\). For a size-\(d\)
1065	  subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) let \(T_I \subseteq T\) be the locus where
1066	  the composite
1067	  \[
1068	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
1069	      \overset{\ \iota_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
1070	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
1071	  \]
1072	  with \(\iota_I\) the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, is an
1073	  isomorphism. This locus is open: in a local trivialisation of \(\mathcal{F}\)

1075	  of its determinant. The \(T_I\) cover \(T\): at any point \(t\), the
1076	  \(d\)-dimensional fibre quotient \(q(t) : \kappa(t)^{r} \twoheadrightarrow
1077	  \mathcal{F}\otimes\kappa(t)\) is surjective, so some \(d\) of the \(r\) standard
1078	  basis vectors map to a basis of the \(d\)-dimensional fibre, i.e.\ \(t \in T_I\)
1079	  for that \(I\) (by Nakayama the isomorphism then holds in a neighbourhood).
1080

1082	  becomes a surjection \(q_I : \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,d}\)

1091	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).

1095	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) of \(\mathcal{U}\)
1096	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}); concretely
1097	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
1098	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). Hence
1099	  \(\varphi_I\) and \(\varphi_J\) land in the overlap charts and agree there after
1100	  the transition \(\theta_{I,J}\), so by the cocycle condition
1101	  (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) the \(\varphi_I\) glue to a single morphism
1102	  \(\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}). By
1103	  construction \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle = \langle \mathcal{F},
1104	  q \rangle\), since this holds on each \(T_I\) and the equivalence class is
1105	  determined by \(\ker(q)\).

\begin{definition}[The glued presheaf of modules: compatible families of chart sections]
  \label{def:gr_modules_gluePresheaf}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.gluePresheaf}
  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}
  With the descent data of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} -- a glue datum \(D\) with
  charts \(U_i\), overlaps \(V_{ij}\), immersions \(f_{ij}\) and transitions
  \(t_{ij}\), glued scheme \(X = D.\mathrm{glued}\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) with
  canonical immersions \(\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\), per-chart sheaves
  \(\mathcal{M}_i\) and transition isomorphisms \(g_{ij}\) -- define, for each open
  \(V \subseteq X\), a \emph{compatible family} over \(V\) to be a tuple
  \((s_i)_{i \in J}\) with \(s_i \in \Gamma(\mathcal{M}_i, \iota_i^{-1}V)\) for every
  \(i\), subject to the overlap relation: over each pairwise overlap \(V_{ij}\) the
  transition isomorphism \(g_{ij} : f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i \xrightarrow{\sim}
  (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\) carries the restriction of \(s_i\) (read as a
  section of \(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) through the chart-restriction bridge
  \cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}) to the corresponding restriction of \(s_j\).
  The assignment
  \[
    V \;\longmapsto\; \bigl\{\,(s_i)_i \in \textstyle\prod_i \Gamma(\mathcal{M}_i,
      \iota_i^{-1}V) \;\big|\; \text{overlap-compatible via } (g_{ij})\,\bigr\},
  \]
  with restriction maps acting componentwise (\(s_i \mapsto s_i|_{\iota_i^{-1}W}\) for
  \(W \subseteq V\)), is a presheaf on \(X\): componentwise restriction preserves the
  overlap relation because restriction is functorial and each \(g_{ij}\) is natural.
  This is the underlying presheaf of the glued sheaf.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}[The \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module structure on the glued presheaf]
  \label{def:gr_modules_gluePresheafModule}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.gluePresheafModule}
  \uses{def:gr_modules_gluePresheaf}
  The presheaf of \cref{def:gr_modules_gluePresheaf} is a presheaf of
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules: over each open \(V \subseteq X\) the set of compatible
  families is an \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-submodule of the product
  \(\prod_i \Gamma(\mathcal{M}_i, \iota_i^{-1}V)\), where \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) acts on
  the \(i\)-th component through the restriction homomorphism
  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V) \to \mathcal{O}_{U_i}(\iota_i^{-1}V)\) induced by \(\iota_i\) and
  the \(\mathcal{M}_i\)-module structure. The overlap relation is preserved by addition
  and by scalar multiplication because each \(g_{ij}\) is \(\mathcal{O}\)-linear and the
  restriction-of-scalars maps commute with the chart immersions; the restriction maps
  of the presheaf are \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear for the same reason. This is the
  pointwise module structure assembled from the chart module structures.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[The glued presheaf is a sheaf]
  \label{lem:gr_modules_gluePresheaf_isSheaf}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.gluePresheafIsSheaf}
  \uses{def:gr_modules_gluePresheaf, def:gr_modules_gluePresheafModule,
    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib, def:gr_glued_scheme}
  The presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules of
  \cref{def:gr_modules_gluePresheaf,def:gr_modules_gluePresheafModule} satisfies the
  sheaf condition: for any open \(V \subseteq X\) and any open cover
  \(\{V_\alpha\}\) of \(V\), a family of compatible families
  \((s^\alpha)_\alpha\) that agree on the pairwise intersections
  \(V_\alpha \cap V_\beta\) amalgamates to a unique compatible family over \(V\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:gr_modules_gluePresheaf, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
  Gluing is checked componentwise. Fix a chart index \(i\); the \(i\)-th components
  \((s^\alpha_i)_\alpha\) form a compatible family of sections of the sheaf
  \(\mathcal{M}_i\) over the cover \(\{\iota_i^{-1}V_\alpha\}\) of \(\iota_i^{-1}V\)
  (their agreement on overlaps follows by restricting the hypothesis to the chart), so
  by the sheaf gluing axiom for \(\mathcal{M}_i\)
  (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) they amalgamate to a unique
  \(s_i \in \Gamma(\mathcal{M}_i, \iota_i^{-1}V)\). The tuple \((s_i)_i\) is again
  overlap-compatible: the defining relation via \(g_{ij}\) is an equality of sections
  over \(V_{ij}\), it holds after restricting to each \(\iota_i^{-1}V_\alpha\)
  (where it is the compatibility of \(s^\alpha\)), and equality of sheaf sections is
  local, so it holds over \(V_{ij}\). Uniqueness of the amalgamation is the separation
  half of the same sheaf condition applied componentwise. Hence the glued presheaf is a
  sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
\end{proof}

\emph{Construction.} The category \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of sheaves of
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has all limits, and both pushforward along a morphism and
  these limits preserve the sheaf condition; the glued sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is therefore
  built directly as a limit -- an \emph{equalizer of pushforwards} -- rather than through a
  hand-built presheaf. Concretely, form the two parallel maps of sheaves of
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
  \[
    \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
      \;\;\underset{b}{\overset{a}{\rightrightarrows}}\;\;
    \prod_{i,j} (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\,\bigl(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr),
  \]
  where, on the \((i,j)\)-component, leg \(a\) pushes the \(i\)-th factor forward along the
  overlap immersion via the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction
  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} packaging the iterated pushforward
  \((\iota_i)_{*} \cong (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*} \circ f_{ij}^{*}\)-comparison), and leg
  \(b\) pushes the \(j\)-th factor forward and additionally transports it across the
  transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\) -- equivalently its inverse \((g_{ij})^{-1}\) -- and the
  glue datum's compatibility identity, so that both legs land on the same overlap sheaf. The
  glued sheaf is the equalizer
  \[
    \mathcal{M} \;=\; \operatorname{eq}(a,b) \;\hookrightarrow\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i,
  \]

  and it satisfies the sheaf condition \cref{lem:gr_modules_gluePresheaf_isSheaf}, the
  amalgamation being computed componentwise from the chart sheaf conditions. Packaging
  the resulting sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is the object \(\mathcal{M} =
  D.\mathrm{glued}.\mathrm{Modules}\) returned here. This specialises effective descent
  of sheaves of modules to the concrete open cover \(\{\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\}\)
  supplied by a scheme glue datum, and is the form in which the Grassmannian's
  tautological bundle and quotient are built.
input.new_string:
\emph{Construction.} The category \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of sheaves of
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has all limits, and both pushforward along a morphism and
  these limits preserve the sheaf condition; the glued sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is therefore
  built directly as a limit -- an \emph{equalizer of pushforwards} -- rather than through a
  hand-built presheaf of compatible families. Concretely, form the two parallel maps of
  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
  \[
    \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
      \;\;\underset{b}{\overset{a}{\rightrightarrows}}\;\;
    \prod_{i,j} (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\,\bigl(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr),
  \]
  where, on the \((i,j)\)-component, leg \(a\) pushes the \(i\)-th factor forward along the
  overlap immersion \(f_{ij}\) using the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction
  together with the pushforward-composition comparison
  \((\iota_i)_{*} \cong (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\circ f_{ij}^{*}\) (the dual of
  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}), and leg \(b\) pushes the \(j\)-th factor forward and
  additionally transports it across the inverse transition isomorphism \((g_{ij})^{-1}\)
  and the glue datum's compatibility identity, so that both legs land on the common overlap
  sheaf \((\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i)\). The glued sheaf is the
  equalizer
  \[
    \mathcal{M} \;=\; \operatorname{eq}(a,b) \;\hookrightarrow\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i,
  \]
  the sub-sheaf of tuples of pushed-forward chart sections whose two overlap restrictions
  agree -- precisely the compatible families, now realised as a categorical limit rather than
  a bespoke presheaf. The cocycle conditions (C1)/(C2) on the family \((g_{ij})\) are
  \emph{not} consumed in forming this equalizer object: the limit exists for any family of
  transition maps. They are instead what make the equalizer descend correctly, and are
  exactly the hypotheses pinned down downstream when the restriction isomorphisms
  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso} are produced. This specialises effective descent
  of sheaves of modules to the concrete open cover \(\{\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\}\)
  supplied by a scheme glue datum, and is the form in which the Grassmannian's
  tautological bundle and quotient are built.

\section{The \(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transition cocycle}
\label{sec:grquot_bundle_cocycle}

The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) is
obtained by feeding the gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} the per-chart free
rank-\(d\) sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\), the bundle transitions
\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the two module cocycle hypotheses (C1)/(C2) that primitive
requires. The matrix cocycle for the Cramer inverses \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) is already in hand --
it is the content of the scheme-level cocycle \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} and the minor identities
\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities} -- but the gluing primitive consumes its
\emph{module-level} incarnation: an honest isomorphism of sheaves of modules together with
its self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity. The three blocks of this section
package exactly that, decoupling the construction of \(\mathcal{U}\) from the remaining
matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping. This is the only net-new infrastructure the
universal quotient requires beyond the gluing primitive itself.

\begin{definition}[The bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\)]
  \label{def:gr_bundleTransition}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition}
  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
    lem:gr_minorDet_unit, def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_transition}
  Let \(I, J \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) be size-\(d\) subsets and let
  \(f_{IJ} : U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\) and \(t_{IJ} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\) be
  the overlap immersion and transition of the chart cover (\cref{def:gr_transition}). The
  \emph{bundle transition} \(g_{I,J}\) is the isomorphism of sheaves of modules on the
  overlap \(U^I_J\)
  \[
    g_{I,J} \;:\;
      f_{IJ}^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\bigr)
      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
      (t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI})^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_{U^J}^{\,d}\bigr)
  \]
  induced by the invertible \(d \times d\) matrix
  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in \mathrm{GL}_d(R^I_J)\) (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}),
  the Cramer inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor, which is invertible because
  \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\) (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}).

  \emph{Realisation.} The transition is built exactly as the chart quotient
  \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}: each entry \((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q} \in R^I_J\) is a global
  function on the overlap, its scalar endomorphism
  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q}\bigr)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the
  \((p,q)\)-component, and the universal property of the rank-\(d\) biproduct assembles
  these into an automorphism of the free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\); the
  source and target pullbacks of free sheaves are identified with this
  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\) through the free-pullback comparisons
  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} along \(f_{IJ}\) and \(t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI}\), conjugating
  the matrix automorphism into the displayed isomorphism. Invertibility of the matrix makes
  \(g_{I,J}\) an isomorphism.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)]
  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_self}
  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
  The self-transition is the identity: \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\) on
  \(f_{II}^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\). This is the module-level form of the descent
  condition (C1) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
  The \(I\)-th minor of the universal matrix is the identity block, \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\)
  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), so its Cramer inverse is again the
  identity, \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\) (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}).
  Hence in the biproduct presentation of \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition} the diagonal
  components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
  off-diagonal components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}), so
  the assembled automorphism is the identity of \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_I}^{\,d}\); conjugation by
  the free-pullback comparisons preserves the identity, giving \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Triple-overlap multiplicativity of the bundle transition (C2)]
  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle}
  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); this is the hard step of the bundle cocycle.
  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_cocycle,
    lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,
    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:modules_pullbackComp}
  Over each triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\) the base-change transports
  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three bundle transitions satisfy
  \[
    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J},
  \]
  the module-level form of the descent condition (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for
  the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\). This is the \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued multiplicative cocycle
  of \(\mathcal{U}\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_cocycle,
    lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,
    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:modules_pullbackComp}
  At the matrix level the assertion is the cocycle identity for the Cramer inverses,
  \[
    (X^J_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}
    \qquad \text{on } V_{IJK},
  \]
  which follows from the minor multiplicativity underlying the scheme-level cocycle
  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} -- in the explicit matrix form
  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq} -- together with the inverse identities
  \cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}. To upgrade this matrix equation to the stated
  equality of sheaf-of-module isomorphisms, the three transitions
  \(g_{IJ}, g_{JK}, g_{IK}\), which live a priori on three different pairwise overlaps, are
  transported to the common triple overlap \(V_{IJK}\) by
  \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}; their source, middle, and target sheaves
  are matched up by the endpoint bridges \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}, and the iterated
  pullbacks are reassociated by the pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}.
  Because the biproduct assembly of \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition} turns matrix multiplication
  into composition of scalar-endomorphism components, the transported composite
  \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}\) realises the left-hand matrix
  product and \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\) realises the right-hand side, so the matrix identity
  yields the module equality. The substantive difficulty is the endpoint bookkeeping: aligning
  the three transports' domains and codomains through the base-change transport and the bridges
  so that the composite typechecks against \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\).
\end{proof}
